title: Today's AI Brief: How Endava builds an agentic organization with Codex
summary: A look at Endava's use of Codex and Google's I/O 2026 moments

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's AI Brief. I've got a couple of interesting stories to share with you. First up, we're looking at how Endava is using Codex to build what's called an agentic organization. The idea here is that by leveraging AI, specifically OpenAI's Codex, Endava aims to accelerate software delivery and significantly cut down the time required for requirements analysis - from weeks down to just hours. This caught my attention because it's a pretty tangible example of AI's potential to change the way we work.

Endava's approach with Codex is about harnessing AI to make software development more efficient. By automating parts of the development process, they can focus on higher-level tasks and deliver software faster. It's fascinating to see how AI is being used to streamline processes that used to be time-consuming and labor-intensive. And it's not just Endava - Google's recent I/O 2026 conference showcased some impressive AI advancements, including new models and updates to Search. Twelve major moments, to be exact - that's a lot to take in, but it's clear that AI is a major player in the tech world right now.

How Endava builds an agentic organization with Codex is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: Learn how Endava uses Codex to build an agentic organization, accelerating software delivery and reducing requirements analysis from weeks to hours. The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

Catch up on 12 major I/O 2026 moments is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: Catch up on 12 major I/O 2026 moments May 28, 2026 · Share x.com Facebook LinkedIn Mail Copy link Relive some of our top onstage moments this year, including the debut of our newest models, updates to Search and more. The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

My quick read is that today's set of stories is useful because it gives us texture, not a sermon. Each item has its own little job: one tells us what changed, another tells us what people are worried about, and another gives the day its mood.
So that is the brief version: a few headlines, a little context, and just enough connective tissue to make the day easier to follow without turning the whole thing into homework.
